title: Git Mental Model, Setup, Help, and Repository Safety · Recovery Case: Repository Boundaries
summary: Adopt an unfamiliar repository without overwriting identity, hooks, ignored files, or team policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git repository boundaries increment in a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Repository Boundaries
steps.instruction:
theory case — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Repository Boundaries contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish working tree, git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.

content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Repository Boundaries condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Pro Git, Second Edition — Controls cumulative conceptual sequence from setup through internals, collaboration, tooling, and distributed workflows.

steps.title: Bound Repository Boundaries
steps.instruction:
theory case — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 5.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Repository Boundaries application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish working tree, git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.

content.text: Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. A defensible solution applies Repository Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 5
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every .git path is necessarily a directory containing all repository data. This fails the Repository Boundaries contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Repair the Repository Boundaries defect
steps.instruction:
theory case — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

A teammate acts on this belief: “Every .git path is necessarily a directory containing all repository data.” Which repair addresses the cause?

steps.title: Defend the Repository Boundaries model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Repository Boundaries: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 5.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
